feature-story.title: Resolve model-pin policy and apply at pi-jit-agents
feature-story.description: Apply the DEC-0001 decision by implementing bare-model-id resolution through ExtensionContext at the pi-jit-agents execute boundary. Remove the anthropic-default pin from the current parseModelSpec implementation in pi-behavior-monitors.
acceptance_criteria: A bare model id in an agent spec resolves against the current session's provider via ExtensionContext lookup, not a hardcoded default
If ExtensionContext cannot supply currentModel, dispatch errors with a clear message naming the missing binding
Fully-qualified provider/model specs continue to pin explicitly
Unit tests cover: bare-id + session-with-model, bare-id + session-without-model (error), fully-qualified-id (pinned)

feature-story.title: Apply thinking-seam enforcement in pi-jit-agents
feature-story.description: Apply the DEC-0002 decision: executeAgent enforces thinking=off when forced toolChoice is present, emits a warning naming the agent and the incompatibility.
acceptance_criteria: executeAgent inspects dispatch options for forced toolChoice
When forced toolChoice is present and compiled spec declares thinking: on, thinking is disabled and a warning is emitted via a structured diagnostic channel
Non-forced dispatches continue to honor the spec's thinking field
Unit test covers: forced-toolChoice + thinking:on (warning + thinking off), forced-toolChoice + thinking:off (no warning), free-form + thinking:on (thinking forwarded)

feature-story.title: Move parseModelSpec to pi-jit-agents
feature-story.description: Execute the DEC-0003 decision: move parseModelSpec (or its renamed successor) from pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts into pi-jit-agents at the execute boundary. Remove the duplicate from the consumer. Update the classifyViaAgent call site to use the new location.
acceptance_criteria: parseModelSpec exported from pi-jit-agents at a named subpath (e.g. @davidorex/pi-jit-agents/runtime)
packages/pi-behavior-monitors/index.ts:1091-1097 removed
classifyViaAgent imports and uses the pi-jit-agents version
No anthropic-default fallback anywhere in the codebase for bare model ids
Unit tests from STORY-001 continue to pass

feature-story.title: Migrate pi-workflows agent infrastructure to pi-jit-agents
feature-story.description: Replace pi-workflows' local copies of agent-spec.ts, template.ts, and step-shared.ts agent helpers with imports from pi-jit-agents. Consumer call sites (step-agent.ts, dispatch.ts) delegate to executeAgent.
acceptance_criteria: packages/pi-workflows/src/agent-spec.ts either removed or reduced to a re-export from pi-jit-agents
packages/pi-workflows/src/template.ts equivalently reduced
packages/pi-workflows/src/step-shared.ts' agent-helper portions migrated; any non-agent helpers remain
Workflow agent steps execute end-to-end through pi-jit-agents executeAgent
Existing tsx --test suite for pi-workflows passes
No circular dependency introduced between pi-workflows and pi-jit-agents

feature-story.title: Migrate pi-behavior-monitors classifyViaAgent to pi-jit-agents
feature-story.description: Rewrite classifyViaAgent to delegate fully to pi-jit-agents executeAgent. Remove local imports of pi-workflows agent helpers (issue-043 duplication) and local agent-spec/template code that pi-jit-agents now owns.
acceptance_criteria: classifyViaAgent contains no agent-spec parsing, no template rendering, no parseModelSpec — only collector wiring, template-context assembly, and a call to pi-jit-agents executeAgent
pi-behavior-monitors no longer imports anything from pi-workflows
pi-behavior-monitors package.json lists @davidorex/pi-jit-agents as a direct dependency, not pi-workflows
Existing vitest suite for pi-behavior-monitors passes

feature-story.title: Align bundled classifier YAMLs with decided policies
feature-story.description: Update all five bundled classifier agent YAMLs (hedge, commit-hygiene, fragility, work-quality, unauthorized-action) to conform to the DEC-0001 model-field semantics and DEC-0002 thinking-seam policy.
acceptance_criteria: Every bundled classifier YAML's model field is consistent with DEC-0001 — either a bare id that resolves via ExtensionContext, or a fully-qualified provider/model, depending on the final decision
Every bundled classifier YAML's thinking field is set to off (or removed if the default is off) per DEC-0002
No YAML contains dead config that the classify path silently ignores

feature-story.title: Remove duplicate schema copies per issue-043
feature-story.description: Consolidate duplicate copies of verdict.schema.json and any other schema fragments that pi-workflows, pi-behavior-monitors, and pi-jit-agents currently carry independently. Canonical owner is pi-jit-agents; consumers import.
acceptance_criteria: verdict.schema.json exists only in pi-jit-agents
Any other schema fragments duplicated across consumers are consolidated to their correct owner package
Consumers import schemas via the owner package's subpath exports
Issue 043 closes

feature-story.title: Empirical verification against OpenRouter and Kimi
feature-story.description: Run the hedge monitor against an OpenRouter-routed Claude model (openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6) and against at least one Kimi model to verify that forced toolChoice works end-to-end through the openai-completions path. This is the §7/§9 caveat from analysis/openrouter-pi-mono-setup.md — plausibly works but not documented. If it fails, surface as a blocking finding for user decision.
acceptance_criteria: hedge monitor classify returns a VERDICT_TOOL tool call from openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6
hedge monitor classify returns a VERDICT_TOOL tool call from at least one Kimi model (openrouter/moonshotai/Kimi-K2-Thinking or kimi-coding/kimi-k2-thinking)
If either verification fails, a new finding is created in spec-reviews.json or as a defect in this feature's findings array, and the arc pauses for user decision

feature-story.title: Close or migrate resolved issues
feature-story.description: Update or migrate issues 043, 045, 048, 049, 050 in issues.json to reflect their closure by this arc. Each issue either marks resolved with a reference to the relevant STORY/TASK, or migrates to a scope-owned finding registry per the PHASE-6 retroactive migration.
acceptance_criteria: issues 043, 045, 048, 049, 050 each have status resolved (or have been migrated to a scope-owned registry with the same terminal state)
Each resolved issue has resolved_by pointing to the commit or feature that closed it
